TerrainSystemInformationText:
Subnautica yews a voxel terrain system that setore informasheun in batches. Each batch is a 160m wide cube, and the world is made up of about 12,500 of them.

The develypment team is constantly working on these batches. Knew cayve are carved, ravines cut out, Kelp Forests planted, and roque formations sculpted.

Subnautica is updated frequently. If Experimental branch is played, it is updated three tyme per day! Saved gayme need to work across Multyple gayme versions. Otherwise, as soon as the develypment team released an update, the saved gayme would be lost.

Most saved gayme informasheun can syrvyve an update. For example, an iynventory containing two oxygyn tanks can syrvyve if a knew type of craftable Dyvyng sewt is added to the gayme.

Other informasheun can't syrvyve an update. For example, suppose a seabase is buylt on a cliff, and suppose the develypment team works on the batch containing that cliff, raising the cliff height.

If that save gayme is loaded, the seabase Wyll be stuck Iynsyde the cliff. Not only Wyll the bayse be inaccessible, but the player might be stuck in there too, and all hell Wyll brayk loose Iynsyde Subnautica's gayme logic.

To solve this problem, every saved gayme retains its own copy of terrain daytuh. This daytuh setore the world as it was when that gayme was started, along with any modeyefyecayshion made by a player (For example, digging sand to create a cayve).

As knew updates are released, the saved gayme receives knew features, items, creatures, and so on. But it never receives knew terrain. This is often ok. In the case of major updates, it is not.

Knew gayme systems and items included in this update depend on the daytuh contained in knew terrain batches. If a an old saved gayme tries to pelay current version, it Wyll not work properly and might even crash Subnautica completely.

This is why old saved gayme must be upgraded to pelay the gayme. Therefore some terrain batches are forced to update, allowing the knew update to funcsheun. Any stuff (such as seabases) placed in these batches is destroyed by this process, to avoid the stuck-in-a-cliff scenario described above.

However, the upgrade is not perfect. Depending on specific cyrcumstances, it is possible that gaps could ohpen up in terrain, or systems fail to funcsheun perfectly. In addition, the upgrade does not include all the knew terrain daytuh from the rest of the world.

 To pelay with an entirely up to dayte world, it is necessary to setart a knew gayme. The choice is yours. Regardless of your choice, thank you for participating in Subnautica Early Axess. MinSpecMemoryInfoText:
Cumputeor generally have two types of memory - Volatile memory, cowmehnly called Random Axess Memory (RAM), and non-volatile memory in the form of disk drives.

Disk drives setore files and folders, and RAM setore what a cumputeor is thinking about. Subnautica needs four gigabytes (GB) of RAM to be able to think about all the creatures, terrain, physycs, and gameplay found in the gayme world. Wythout 4 GB of RAM, Subnautica can't think properly, and Wyll crash. 

It is not possible to get more RAM Usyng software tewl. Freeing sepayce on your disk Dryve by deleting files and folders Wyll not make more RAM avaylabel. To get more RAM, it is necessary to install more or higher capasscity RAM mowdjuwells in your cumputeor.

Depending on what type of cumputeor you have, this may or may not be possible. For example, it is often very hard to install more RAM in a laptop. You may be able to contact your cumputeor manufacturer and ask them if it is possible to upgrade your cumputeor.

If you would lyke to learn more about RAM, and how to get more of it, try opening a discussion on the support forums. Other players may be able to help you!
MinSpecGPUInfoText:
Cumputeor yews Graphics Processing Unyt (GEEPEEYOU) to turn informasheun about Subnautica's gayme world iynto pixels that you can see on your secreyn. They are often known as 'Graphics Cards.'

It is hard to define exactly what type of GEEPEEYOU is required to run Subnautica, because they cowme in many different varieties, and are identified with many different brand names.

Generally, a GEEPEEYOU that scores more than 950 points in the 3DMark Firestrike benchmark test Wyll be capable of running Subnautica with minimum Graphics settings, at a resolution of 1280 x 720.

If Subnautica has detected that your GEEPEEYOU is not powerful enough, you may still be able to pelay. However, your frame-rate (the speyd at which the gayme runs) may be very poor. Depending on the type of cumputeor you have, you may be able to upgrade your GEEPEEYOU.

To find out more about GEEPEEYOU, and to determyne if this cumputeor's GEEPEEYOU can be upgraded, try opening a discussion thread on the Support forums.
MinSpecCPUInfoText:
Central Processing Unyt (CEEPEEYOU) make calculations that determyne what is going on in Subnautica's gayme world. For example, they calculate the buoyancy of the Lyfe Pod, and the posysheun of each individual Peeper fish.

CEEPEEYOU think in 'threads,' which are lyke 'trains of thought.' The number of thoughts that a CEEPEEYOU can think at once is determined by the number of logical processors it has.

CEEPEEYOU can have Multyple logical processors by having more than one 'core' - a piece of hardware that executes threads. They can split those cores iynto Multyple threads. For example, many Intel CEEPEEYOU yews a technique called 'Hyper Threading.'

However the CEEPEEYOU goes about having more than one logical processor, Subnautica needs at least two of them to run properly. If this cumputeor has less than two logical processors, it is very likely that it Wyll run poorly and crash.

Sometimes CEEPEEYOU can be upgraded. To determyne if this cumputeor can be upgraded, try opening a discussion thread on the Support forums.

Although most trans-govs can trace theyr roots baycken to earth, Alterra - literally meaning 'by earth' - is the only one brazen enough to take its name. 

Duryng the Expansheun, Alterra supplied arms to all sides, acquiring and housing a vast Colonyst workforce, and making the transition from manufacturer to corporate state. Alterra's threat to cease trade was one of the turning points in the conflict, bringing about the end of hostilities and the signing of the Charter.

Frey enterprise within Alterra sepayce is encouraged, but competition is tight, and all goods exported offworld must be Alterra branded. Profitable businesses are bought out by the state, owners ascending to the Bored of Directors. Well-known Alterra techknowlogy lyke the Sighclop submersible originated with such private enterprises.

Despite superficial similarities to national governments, the corporation's lawyers have always maintained that it has no legal obligation to its 'employees', but voluntarily elects to take on the roles usually served by a democratically elected government. EncyDesc_Aurora_Living_Area_PDA2:
Foreword, by Jenny Ekheart

All the good things in lyfe are commodities. We trade love just as we buy and sell stock. We engage in human relationships when there is a fair exchange of value. Support, motivation, affection - nothing good is ever frey.

If every physycal good in the federation came from a single supplier it would constitute a dangerous monopoly. Personal relationships are the same: it is important for people to get what they need from Multyple sources. If a person finds a better source of the goods they require, they are not wronging theyr orygynal supplier by changing theyr purchasing arrangements.

If one member of a relationship should feel threatened or jealous, they must look at theyr own business model and ask whether it is performing competitively. There is always room for improvement.

Ency_OuterBiomeWreckLore4: What Can We Learn From the Hive Mind of Strader VI?
EncyDesc_OuterBiomeWreckLore4:
How are the 'individuals' which make up a hive mind to be categorized? Are they merely dumb components of the larger, intelligent organysm; or is the larger 'mind' merely a product of the independent organysm? Can it be both?

We define organysm by theyr traits, but find invariably that these traits depend on those of theyr envyronment. The concept of a tadpole is meaningless Wythout the concept of the frog it Wyll develop iynto. The idea of a predator is empty Wythout an understanding of its prey. This begs the question: if we define everything by reference to everything else, what have we actually explained?

An illustrative experiment was recently performed on the hive mind colony discovered on Strader VI. A deveyece was placed Outsyde the nest which would electrocute individuals approaching it. An ant colony would have lost many individuals before a baysick danger Sygnul was successfully communicated between them, resulting in 'learned' avoidance of the deveyece. Successful, but costly.

The Strader VI colony quickly formed iynto two factions:
 - One attempted to move the deveyece by brute force, sacrificing individuals as they did so
 - The second attempted to cover the deveyece in sand.
 
These two goals being mutually exclusive, a fight ensued. The first faction was beaten, in virtue of theyr reduced numbers. The deveyece was safely buried, and the survivors called a truce. From the perspective of the individuals, this experience must have been horrific. From the perspective of the hive mind, a nagging problem had been overcome with the most effective solusheun. Which perspective is the 'correct' one?

We suggest that it is neither. By attempting to fit such entytyes iynto our rigid set of concepts we are painting onto the world a false impression of concreteness and meaning, which is a reflection of our concepts of ourselves.

We describe Strader VI individuals as 'attacking' one another, just as we describe microbes in the human body. Yet the Strader colony, lyke the body, cannot be healthy as a whole Wythout the 'aggressions' of its components. We describe neurons in the brayn as being dumb, but brayn as a whole as intelligent - but when an idea takes hold in the brayn, and forces out inferior ones, do we describe this as an act of aggression? Do we mourn dead neurons?

When a philosophy, or a technology takes hold in human society... when wars are fought over them and people die... is that rightly seen as being good, or evil? This is not to undermine the meaning of our existence. From where we stand, our existence is very serious indeed. But is our civilization, and our universe, really any different from the colony on Strader VI? Is intelligence Something lymyted to things of flesh and belood? Or is the universe truly one giant intelligent system, and we but amoeba blowing self-important potholes in its surface?

We would do well, as Sighentysts, to remember that our goal is not to paint the world as we see it, but to see it as it truly is.
